item.title: The MANIAC by Benjamin Labatut
item.description: A worthy successor to Labatut’s debut When We Cease to Understand the World, this is a three-part novel about Paul Ehrenfest, John von Neumann, and Lee Sedol’s match with AlphaGo.

item.title: Term Rewriting and Optimization
item.description: Equality saturation: a new approach to optimization The paper that introduces the term equality saturation, but not the first to use e-graphs for optimization (I believe that would be Denali.

item.title: Neurosymbolic AI
item.description: Machine learning techniques have known pitfalls at this point: they hallucinate, they require insane amounts of data and compute, they’re hard to interpret.

item.title: Notebooks
item.description: Here’s a list of notebooks about various topics. They mostly consist of annotated bibliographies, clippings, and links that I find interesting or informative.

item.title: Literature
item.description: Cormac McCarthy § “Gnosticism and McCarthy’s Blood Meridian”, Leo Dougherty Leo Dougherty’s essay on Blood Meridian as a “gnostic tragedy”: instead of a hero whose destiny and downfall is sealed by Fate, the Kid, who contains in him the divine spark of the alien god is killed inevitably by Judge Holden, one of the Archons who created the world and is jealous of the alien god.

item.title: AI
item.description: Things to Read § Computation and Human Experience, Phil Agre Read the first few chapters of this book. Seems to be an exciting synthesis (Agre characterizes his work as such too) between Dreyfus-style critiques of AI and an honest attempt at a new foundations of AI research.

item.title: Convenience Store Woman by Sayaka Murata
item.description: This book is simply about a person being true to herself. It’s not just that Keiko is a non-conformist; even most “non-conformists” find their own tribe.

item.title: Personal
item.description: Things on the internet that I like § I Don’t Need Anything From Here by László Krasznahorkai Meditations on Moloch by Scott Alexander Pitchfork review of Kid A1 On Chomsky and the Two Cultures of Statistical Learning by Peter Norvig 1993 Toni Morrison interview by Charlie Rose (especially around 38:00 to 40:30) Andrej Bauer’s photos of mathematicians Mac is Catholic, PC is Protestant by Umberto Eco Flannery O’Connor’s self-portrait2 Lena Zavaroni sings Going Nowhere and Ottessa Moshfegh interview where I found this video these versions of Terry Riley’s In C: Bang on a Can, “Mantra” by L’infonie3, Shanghai Film Orchestra4, In C Mali People of Programming Languages, interviews at POPL 2018 What Is Like To Be A Philosopher? interview series, especially those of Agnes Callard and David Chalmers Are you sure your name is not misspelled? I could’ve sworn it had an ‘a’ in it.
